creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Jensen Huang says he’s found a ‘brand new’ $200B market for Nvidia
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: The next big thing for Nvidia will be CPUs for AI agents, $200 billion worth, CEO Jensen Huang predicts.
contentSnippet: The next big thing for Nvidia will be CPUs for AI agents, $200 billion worth, CEO Jensen Huang predicts.

creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Anthropic says it’s about to have its first profitable quarter
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: Anthropic has told its investors that it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in its second quarter.
contentSnippet: Anthropic has told its investors that it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in its second quarter.

title: (No AI) In SpaceX’s IPO, Elon Musk is a risk factor
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![SpaceX rocket](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/gettyimages-2270729873.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

[The SpaceX IPO is here](https://www.theverge.com/business/902219/spacex-ipo-details), and it's more than just an historic public offering that could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. It also reveals more ways in which Elon Musk's companies interact and overlap with each other, shuffling money around in ways that are often difficult to keep track of.

This is evident in ways that are both obvious and less so. A CTRL-F search for "Tesla" yields 87 results, xAI is mentioned 356 times, and X 267 times. Even the Boring Company (7 times) and Neuralink (3) get a few mentions. Throughout its 330 pages of rocket launches and interplanetary wishes, you can trace the network of ways in which Musk's …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/science/935102/spacex-ipo-elon-musk-tesla-cybertruck-xai-risk-factor)

It also reveals more ways in which Elon Musk's companies interact and overlap with each other, shuffling money around in ways that are often difficult to keep track of. 
This is evident in ways that are both obvious and less so. A CTRL-F search for "Tesla" yields 87 results, xAI is mentioned 356 times, and X 267 times. Even the Boring Company (7 times) and Neuralink (3) get a few mentions. Throughout its 330 pages of rocket launches and interplanetary wishes, you can trace the network of ways in which Musk's  …

title: (No AI) ‘Solve all diseases,’ you say?
author: Victoria Song
content:
![A picture of Demis Hassabis onstage at Google I/O 2026 with the screen behind him saying “A new golden age of scientific discovery”](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/demishassabis.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Let’s unpack what Demis Hassabis said at the end of yesterday’s Google I/O keynote.

_This is_ [Optimizer](https://www.theverge.com/optimizer-newsletter)_, a weekly newsletter sent from_ Verge _senior reviewer_ [_Victoria Song_](https://www.theverge.com/authors/victoria-song) _that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. This week's issue is a special early edition tied to_ The Verge's _Google I/O coverage. You can expect our next issue at its usual time next Friday. Opt in for_ Optimizer _[here](https://www.theverge.com/newsletters)._

Toward the end of this year's Google I/O keynote, Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis declared, with a completely deadpan face, that the company hopes to "reimagine the drug discovery process with the goal of one day solving all disease."

This is the sort of statement that the phras …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/935021/google-io-gemini-for-science-alphafold-alphagenome-ai-health)

title: (No AI) The next phase of OpenAI’s Education for Countries
content: OpenAI advances Education for Countries, expanding AI adoption in schools with new partnerships, teacher training, and tools to improve global learning outcomes.
contentSnippet: OpenAI advances Education for Countries, expanding AI adoption in schools with new partnerships, teacher training, and tools to improve global learning outcomes.

title: (No AI) An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry
content: An OpenAI model solved the 80-year-old unit distance problem, disproving a major conjecture in discrete geometry and marking a milestone in AI-driven mathematics.
contentSnippet: An OpenAI model solved the 80-year-old unit distance problem, disproving a major conjecture in discrete geometry and marking a milestone in AI-driven mathematics.

title: (No AI) Fast-tracking genetic leads to reverse cellular aging
content: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.
contentSnippet: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.

title: (No AI) Simulate real-world places with Project Genie and Street View
content: We’re expanding access to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally and introducing a new capability powered by Street View.
contentSnippet: We’re expanding access to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally and introducing a new capability powered by Street View.

creator: Maxwell Zeff, Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) SpaceX Listed Grok’s ‘Spicy’ Mode as a Risk in Its IPO Filing
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff, Paresh Dave
content: The rocket company has set aside more than $500 million for potential litigation losses, in part to account for complaints alleging that Grok created sexualized images.
contentSnippet: The rocket company has set aside more than $500 million for potential litigation losses, in part to account for complaints alleging that Grok created sexualized images.

creator: Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) SpaceX Is Spending $2.8 Billion to Buy Gas Turbines for Its AI Data Centers
dc:creator: Paresh Dave
content: The investment comes as Elon Musk’s AI unit faces complaints about the carbon-emitting units and looks to become a big player in cloud computing.
contentSnippet: The investment comes as Elon Musk’s AI unit faces complaints about the carbon-emitting units and looks to become a big player in cloud computing.
